summary: High‑energy Help‑Slip‑Franklin stretch and tight second set
blurb: Help On The Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower rockets the second set, followed by a powerful Playing In The Band and The Other One. Phil’s bass is front‑and‑center, driving a tight groove throughout, while Jerry delivers soaring guitar tones despite occasional vocal weakness. Overall the show packs high‑energy jams and solid band cohesion.
review: September 12, 1985 at the Henry J. Kaiser Convention Center captured one of those rare moments when the Dead’s second set just takes off. The Help On The Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower sequence is a scorcher – Jerry “bouncing around the rafters” with soaring lead lines, Brent (though not singled out) laying down lush keys, and Phil’s bass cutting through like a laser. That momentum rolls straight into a tight‑grooving Playing In The Band that segues into an electrifying stretch of The Other One, keeping the audience on its feet. While the first set has a few rushed spots and Garcia’s vocals sound a bit thin on the matrix mix, Phil’s articulate low end and the overall high‑energy vibe keep everything locked in. The solid SBD capture (Barbella) showcases the band’s fire without any distracting technical flaws, making this show a standout entry from the mid‑’80s era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the Help‑Slip‑Franklin stretch, Playing In The Band and The Other One as highlights; Phil’s bass is repeatedly highlighted as a major strength, and Jerry’s guitar work is described as soaring. Only minor negatives (first‑set quirks, weak vocals) appear in one mixed review, so overall sentiment is strongly positive.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with good, listenable quality that captures the high‑energy performance and standout jams without noticeable technical issues.

key_highlights: Scorching Help On The Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower sequence.
Tight, driving Playing In The Band leading into an electrifying The Other One.
Phil Lesh’s articulate bass anchors the high‑energy second set.
Warm audience vibe captured on a solid SBD recording; overall sound quality good.

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring guitar tones and a flying spirit throughout; vocals are weak on some mixes but jam space remains tasty.
band_performance.Phil: Very present, articulated bass that drives the band's synchronicity and tight groove.
